title: Select a vector database for the workload
description: Choose among pgvector, Qdrant, and Milvus based on scale, filtering needs, and operational fit.
body: Use when picking where to store and search vectors. Do not default to a standalone vector DB when the corpus is small and already in Postgres — pgvector may suffice and cut operational load. Actions: estimate vector count and QPS, list metadata-filtering and hybrid needs, then match: pgvector for modest scale beside relational data, Qdrant/Milvus for large-scale ANN with rich filtering. Validation signals include a benchmark at expected scale meeting the recall and latency budget and a filtering test on real metadata. Risk boundary: outgrowing pgvector's index causes latency cliffs; if projected scale exceeds its comfort zone, escalate the decision before committing the storage layer.
